headline_seed: Ally of DOJ pardon attorney seeks to join board of Trump's $1.7+ billion fund - CBS News
draft_text:
The DOJ created a $1.776B "Anti-Weaponization Fund" from a settlement of Trump's IRS lawsuit. Per NBC News and Time Magazine, anyone may apply — including Jan. 6 defendants. Claims close December 15, 2028.

And that's the mews.
